text: urinal rectocin moidectomy, Altemeyer's procedure for the treatment of full thickness rectal prolapse.
The patient is under general anesthesia and positioned in lithotomy. A long star retractor is in place to assist with the surgical procedure.
The surgeon uses Allis clamps to fully evert the prolapsed rectum. A circumferential incision is made with electrocautery approximately one to two centimeters above the dentate line.

The surgeon circumferentially sections the rectum using a ligature blunt tip, reaching the peritoneum of the pouch of Douglas. The ventral side of the prolapsed bowel is visible as the pouch is opened, requiring careful maneuvering to avoid perforation of the adjacent vaginal or bladder structures.
The surgeon draws the elongated bowel through the opening in the peritoneum, then sections the mesocolon using a ligature blunt tip at the chosen site for colic section.
The surgeon identifies the levator ani muscles and performs a posterior levatoroplasty, using interrupted absorbable sutures to enhance postoperative continence.
The surgeon references the anal canal in a cardinal manner and progressively excises the prolapsed bowel using electrocautery.

The surgeon performs a Hans-Jun coloanal anastomosis by suturing the proximal margin of the bowel to the anal canal using interrupted 3-0 absorbable sutures. The anastomosis is completed with additional interrupted sutures, securing the connection between the bowel and anal canal.
The Lone Star retractor is removed from the surgical site.
